column6: If the measured branching fraction is used literally, this partial width implies a total width of roughly 0.23 MeV. This is compatible with a very narrow Ds1(2460) and is not in tension with experiment.
If the measured branching fraction is used literally, this partial width implies a total width of roughly 0.09 MeV. This is also compatible with a very narrow Ds1(2460).
Dividing by the measured total width gives an estimated radiative branching fraction of about 0.6%, so this channel is expected to be experimentally small.
Dividing by the measured total width gives an estimated radiative branching fraction of about 0.07%, reinforcing the cancellation-suppressed picture.

interpretation: No direct radiative measurement; CMS/LHCb observe Bs1(5830) mainly in B(*)K spectroscopy
column6: This is a prediction for future searches. Without a measured radiative branching fraction or precise total width, the comparison is qualitative.
